assumptions: The developer may not yet have a Microsoft account, Azure subscription, or Entra tenant.
No application type, runtime, tenant kind, or identity outcome has been selected.

developer_goal: Create the required Microsoft identity tenancy and reach the first Entra-backed sign-in or token success for a chosen application architecture.

first_success.outcome: The selected Microsoft identity platform quickstart reaches its documented first authentication or token result.
first_success.observable_signal: Depends on the unresolved outcome; for example, redirect back to an ASP.NET Core app after sign-in without an Entra error.

route_selection.surface: Microsoft Entra admin center and Microsoft identity platform quickstarts
route_selection.rule: Select the shortest complete route only after identity outcome, tenant kind, and application type are fixed.

candidates.name: Interactive web app sign-in
candidates.reason: Web sign-in is one peer starting outcome on the identity platform documentation home.

candidates.name: Single-page application sign-in
candidates.reason: SPA platform configuration and token handling differ from confidential web apps.

candidates.name: Daemon or service application-only tokens
candidates.reason: Application-only flows change credentials, permissions, and the success signal away from interactive sign-in.

candidates.name: Workforce tenant versus external tenant
candidates.reason: External tenants add admin-consent and setup steps that workforce Default Directory routes may not require.

required_path.action: Create a free Azure account or otherwise obtain a Microsoft Entra tenant the developer can administer.

required_fields.label: Microsoft account and Azure subscription signup fields
required_fields.notes: Official docs require an Azure account with an active subscription but do not enumerate current signup fields.

required_path.observable_result: The developer can sign in to the Microsoft Entra admin center against a tenant.

required_path.action: Register a new application under Entra ID > App registrations.
required_path.observable_result: The app Overview shows an Application (client) ID.

required_path.action: Configure the platform, redirect URI, and credentials required by the selected app type.

required_fields.label: Credentials or API permissions if required by the route

required_path.observable_result: The registration has the platform settings needed for the chosen quickstart.

required_path.action: Complete the selected runtime quickstart through first sign-in or token success.
required_path.observable_result: The selected sample reaches its documented identity terminal state.

prerequisites.description: An Azure account with an active subscription and at least Application Developer rights is required to register apps.
A workforce or external tenant must exist; Default Directory may be used for the registration quickstart.

external_gates.description: Azure account creation and subscription entitlement sit outside Entra app registration itself.
External tenants may require admin consent for delegated permissions before end users can proceed.

platform_outcomes.description: Microsoft Entra creates the application object and assigns an Application (client) ID on Register.
A service principal is created for the app registration in the tenant.

excluded.item: Making the enterprise app visible on My Apps
excluded.reason: Official registration docs present visibility as a later optional step after registration.

excluded.item: Reading the full integration checklist as a measured action
excluded.reason: Checklist reading is documentation, not a product interaction.

sources.title: Microsoft identity platform documentation
evidence_supported: Peer starting outcomes across app and service scenarios

sources.title: Quickstart: Add sign-in with Microsoft identity to an ASP.NET Core web app
evidence_supported: One example interactive web sign-in route among several

sources.title: Register an application in Microsoft Entra ID
Register an application with the Microsoft identity platform
Microsoft identity platform integration checklist
evidence_supported: Broader integration checklist excluded from first-success measurement

uncertainties.question: Which Entra identity outcome, tenant kind, application type, and runtime define first success?
uncertainties.impact: These choices change registration fields, credentials, consent, implementation work, and the terminal signal.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Bound one goal such as workforce-tenant ASP.NET Core interactive sign-in, then verify Azure signup through first successful redirect.

uncertainties.question: What required fields and verification steps appear on the current free Azure account signup?
uncertainties.impact: Signup field burden and waits cannot be counted without a current public or fresh-account capture.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated walkthrough of azure.microsoft.com free-account signup for a new Microsoft identity.
